# monster-swarm

The **distributed-inference guardrails** of the monster family: UCAN-gated,
cost-admitted **CRUD over a leased swarm of subagents**. This is how Thoth
creates, reads, updates, and deletes distributed-inference nodes in a guarded,
UCAN-enlightened way. Built on the Rust [`a0n-auth`](../../auth/rust) UCAN
crate, the [`monster-guard`](../monster-guard) enforcement path, and the
[`monster-resident`](../monster-resident) capability root.

For the top-down story of the whole accountable-agent lifecycle (the problem, the
principle, and the six gates A-F), see the capstone
[`MONSTER_ACCOUNTABLE_AGENTS`](../MONSTER_ACCOUNTABLE_AGENTS.md).

- **What:** a small Rust binary + library that lets a holder of an `agent/*`
  capability spawn real distributed-inference nodes (e.g. `aeon-monitor`) as
  sandboxed, time-leased subagents, list them, renew/re-attenuate them, and
  reap them — each subagent joining the swarm partyline so the fleet "stays
  talking".
- **Why:** Thoth should be able to grow and prune a swarm of inference workers
  without that power being unbounded. Every mutation is **double-gated**, and
  the runtime kill switch (lease + revoke) is real, not advisory.
- **How:** `spawn` verifies the caller's UCAN, admits the budding event against
  the colony budget, `monster-guard delegate`s a command grant, then launches
  the node under `monster-guard run` (auto-renewing lease). `reap` revokes the
  grant; the node dies at the next lease tick.
- **Verified:** the **Qwen quality gate** passes. `tests/paris_subagent.rs`
  spawns a real `fat-station` as a UCAN-leased subagent on the mesh, drives
  "The capital of France is" through `/decode-next`, and asserts the real
  Qwen coder-7B model predicts ` Paris` (token **12095**) — the real-inference
  ratchet against "fake agent" regressions (~2 min). See the
  [`MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK`](../MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK.md) for how to run it.
- **Done:** the full accountable-agent lifecycle is complete and proven end to
  end. The six gates — A QUALITY (`spawn --qspec`), B CONDUCT (`spawn --oath`),
  C ENFORCEMENT (`oath-breach`), D DELEGATION (`delegate-spawn`),
  E TAMPER-EVIDENCE (`audit --verify`), F CONTAINMENT (`reap --cascade`) — are
  exercised against the real release binaries by
  [`scripts/demo-admission.sh`](./scripts/demo-admission.sh) (six gates, exit 0,
  ~3s) and backed by `cargo test`. All CRUD is surfaced as `subagent_*` /
  `swarm_*` / `audit_*` MCP tools in [`monster-resident`](../monster-resident)
  and is user-spawnable via `moonshine agent spawn`. Per-subagent identity is
  closed (own P-256 DID + keyfile, ECDSA verify + sign loop); trust attaches to
  the stable spawner. See the capstone
  [`MONSTER_ACCOUNTABLE_AGENTS`](../MONSTER_ACCOUNTABLE_AGENTS.md).
- **Done (this round):** the collective swarm consciousness now **auto-triggers
  budding/reaping on a live box** — `auto --interval` runs as a controller daemon
  that reads the partyline and re-forks the fleet within the FRF budget, every
  action a signed audit entry tagged actor "auto", driven/observed with
  `broadcast`. See [Autonomic re-fork (live)](#autonomic-re-fork-live).
- **Next:** wire the zedge passthrough so the controller can re-fork across the
  edge mesh; and ship a CI-cheap dense 0.5B knot so the Paris ratchet can run in
  CI (steps in the runbook).

## The two guardrails

Every **mutation** (`spawn`, `update`, `reap`) is gated twice:

1. **UCAN-enlightened** — the caller presents a capability rooted at the shared
   resident root (`a0n_auth::verify_chain`) that grants `agent/<verb>` on
   `agent:fleet`. A holder granted `agent/*` may do all of CRUD. Bad signature,
   wrong root, revoked, or wrong verb ⇒ rejected.
2. **Cost-admitted** — the op passes the **AntColony FRF budget** (mirrors
   `Gnosis.AntColonyCollectiveIntelligence`):
   - a **population ceiling** (default DECAGON = 10, one slot per bit of the
     10-bit aeon frame the swarm talks over) gates `spawn`;
   - the **two-queen reap floor** (`budding_survives_one_queen_loss`) forbids
     reaping the last persistent breeder, so the colony always survives;
   - the **God-Formula stability weight** (`budget − min(vent, budget) + 1`) is
     reported and is provably always ≥ 1.

Runtime enforcement is then **monster-guard's** job: sandbox + lease + revoke.

The fleet ledger is a **Thoth conversation anti-queue**
(`Gnosis.ThothConversationAntiQueue`): each live subagent is a held lease
("promise"); `reap` is the release; the oldest held promise carries the most
review pressure.

## Quality-gated admission

`spawn` takes an optional **`--qspec <file>`**, threaded straight to the
`monster-guard delegate` shell-out: the subagent is admitted **only if its
quality spec passes**, so a fake or broken artifact never gets a lease and never
runs. (`update` does not re-probe — quality is proven at spawn.) The
[`.moonshine`](../../.moonshine) registry now carries **7 columns**
(`name kind path caps build quality oath`): a **`quality`** column (a qspec path
or `-`) and an **`oath`** column (an oath path or `-`). `setup.sh` passes each on
to `monster-guard delegate` as `--qspec <quality>` / `--oath <oath>`, so a
registered command is admitted **only if its quality probe passes AND its oath
verifies** — a double gate at registration. The reference spec lives at
[`qspecs/paris.qspec.json`](./qspecs/paris.qspec.json); a worked admitted-plugin
example lives at
[`oaths/example-admitted-plugin.md`](./oaths/example-admitted-plugin.md).

This is monster-guard's
[quality-gated admission](../monster-guard/README.md#quality-gated-admission-qspec)
pushed to the spawn boundary — admission proves the thing works. Full operator
detail in the
[`MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK`](../MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK.md#quality-gated-admission-qspec).

## Oaths

Where a qspec gates **quality** (*works*), an **oath** gates **conduct**
(*behaves*). `spawn` takes an optional **`--oath <file>`** (`*.oath.json`:
`{ "oath", "max_caps", "ttl_max_secs", "sworn_by"? }`) threaded straight to
`monster-guard delegate`, which **denies admission** unless the requested caps
are a subset of `max_caps` and ttl ≤ `ttl_max_secs`, then **binds the oath into
the grant as the UCAN `fct`** so the conduct contract travels with the
capability. `spawn --oath` also **records the oath in the ledger** and
**surfaces each agent's oath in the [`thoth.fleet.swarm.v1`](#commands) fleet
frame** — keeping every agent's sworn conduct front of mind in Thoth's
gate/consciousness surface (`ThothGateScore::Oath`), where a conduct monitor
(e.g. the monster-badchild rogue-bodyguard layer) enforces against it. Reference
oaths live at [`oaths/`](./oaths). See
[oath-bound admission](../monster-guard/README.md#oath-bound-admission) and the
[`MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK`](../MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK.md#oaths-and-user-spawnable-agents).

**Signed at spawn (the swearer's signature, not just root's).** An oath can now be
a **signed oath-token** — a UCAN that proves the swearer swore it, not just raw
JSON attested by root. At `spawn`, the agent **self-signs its oath** with its own
freshly-minted keypair: a **Ulysses** token (`iss == aud` — the agent ties itself
to the mast, a self-bound irrevocable precommitment) written to
`$MONSTER_GUARD_HOME/oath-tokens/<id>.oath.ucan` and handed to `monster-guard`
for `verify_chain`-at-admission. A **co-signed** oath is a 2-link chain — the
spawner imposes via `IssueOptions.proofs`, the agent accepts. Because the proof is
now cryptographic, the ledger and the `thoth.fleet.swarm.v1` frame carry
**`oath_signed`** and **`oath_sworn_by`** alongside the oath text — the signature
and swearer are front of mind, not just the prose. Mint a standalone self-signed
oath-token with:

```
monster-swarm oath mint --id <id> --terms <oath.json>
    Self-sign the oath terms into a Ulysses oath-token (iss == aud) under the
    agent's own key; write the *.oath.ucan and print its path.
```

### Enforcement

A sworn oath only bites if breaking it has a consequence. **Enforcement** closes
the lifecycle — **swear -> bind -> prove -> enforce** — by turning a breach into a
**revoke + an append-only audit record**:

```
monster-swarm oath-breach --ucan <token> --id <id> --reason <reason>
    Enforce a breach: REVOKE the agent (its lease is killed at the next tick)
    and APPEND a record to $MONSTER_GUARD_HOME/breaches.json. A guarded mutation
    — needs a UCAN authorizing the reap, exactly like `reap`. Breach reports can
    be signature-verified (sign.rs), so the report is attributable.

monster-swarm breaches [--json]
    List the recorded breaches (read the append-only audit log).
```

The audit row captures both halves of the contract:
`{ id, name, oath_sworn_by, oath, caste, reason, reported_by, ts }` — the oath
that was sworn and the breach that broke it. The log is **append-only** (never
rewritten, only grown).

`auto` enforces on its own cadence with **`--enforce-oaths [--breach-at <n>]`**:
each tick reaps agents whose conduct signal (`SwarmSignal` consciousness **>=
`--breach-at`**, default **5**) has crossed into breach, recording each via the
same `oath-breach` path — the lease-level reflex on a timer.

This is the **lease-level reflex**: it mechanically revokes and records. The
**semantic conduct judgment** — did the agent actually misbehave / exfiltrate —
belongs to the
[`monster-badchild`](../monster-badchild) rogue-bodyguard layer, which reads the
bound oath at runtime and **calls `oath-breach`** to enforce. Full operator
detail (the schema, the badchild conduct-hook contract, the lifecycle) is in the
[`MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK`](../MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK.md#oath-enforcement-breach---revoke--audit).

### Trust

Enforcement makes a single breach bite; **trust** gives breaches memory and
**gates future spawns** by reputation, closing the loop:
**swear -> bind -> prove -> enforce -> score -> gate**. Trust attaches to the
**spawner** (the UCAN holder who requests spawns) — **not** the agent: each agent
gets a fresh DID per spawn so it has no history to score, while the spawner is a
stable identity accountable for the agents it spawns. The breach record gains a
**`spawned_by`** (spawner DID); trust is then
`trust_of(spawner) = clamp(1 - 0.34 * recent_breach_count, 0..1)` (0 breaches ->
1.0; 3+ -> ~0).

```
monster-swarm trust [--json]
    List spawner DIDs with their trust score and recent breach count (the
    reputation view over the breaches.json audit log).
```

**Admission-by-trust.** `ops::spawn` denies a spawn when the requesting spawner's
trust is below a floor (default **0.34**, env **`MONSTER_SWARM_MIN_TRUST`**) — a
repeat offender whose agents keep breaching their oaths is locked out of spawning
new agents until its recent breach count decays back under the floor. Clean
spawners (trust `1.0`) are unaffected. Trust is also surfaced **front of mind** in
the [`thoth.fleet.swarm.v1`](#commands) fleet frame, so the Thoth Oath-gate weighs
*who is asking* alongside *what they have sworn*. A runnable end-to-end walkthrough
(quality + oath + enforcement + trust lockout) lives at
[`scripts/demo-admission.sh`](./scripts/demo-admission.sh). Full operator detail is
in the
[`MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK`](../MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK.md#trust-scoring-reputation-gates-future-spawns).

### Recursive delegation

Trust runs *up* the tree (a child's breach dings its parent); **recursive
delegation** is the matching constraint running *down*: a parent agent may spawn a
CHILD, but delegation **attenuates** — no node hands a child more than it holds.
The swarm is a fully-accountable delegation **tree**, every node UCAN-rooted, caps
and oath bounds strictly narrowing downward.

A parent mints a child's attenuated spawn capability with
**[`delegate-spawn`](#commands)**:

```
monster-swarm delegate-spawn --ucan <parent-token> --issuer-secret <hex> \
                             --to <child-did> --caps <subset> [--ttl <secs>]
```

It mints an attenuated `agent/spawn` cap carrying the constraint `{caps: subset}`;
a0n-auth's `verify_chain` **rejects a non-subset**, and `ops::spawn` independently
enforces the requested sandbox `--caps` are within the authorizing chain's
permitted caps (root `agent/*` is unrestricted; a delegated holder is bounded by
its `{caps}` constraint). Conduct attenuates too: a **co-signed oath chain**
(parent imposes via `IssueOptions.proofs`, child accepts) must not loosen the
parent's bounds — monster-guard denies an **"oath escalation"** if a child's oath
`max_caps` exceed the parent's or its ttl ceiling is higher, and the grant `fct`
records `oath_chain_depth` + `imposed_by`. The **lineage** is recorded too: the
ledger and the [`thoth.fleet.swarm.v1`](#commands) frame carry the chain `depth`
and `spawned_by` (the parent DID), so the tree (root -> Thoth/user -> agent ->
child) is auditable and trust propagates up it. Full operator detail in the
[`MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK`](../MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK.md#recursive-accountability-the-delegation-tree).

## Cascade revocation (containment)

Recursive delegation makes the swarm a **tree**; **cascade revocation** is the
containment guarantee over that tree: reaping or breaching a node reaps its
**entire spawned subtree**, so a revoked or rogue parent never leaves orphaned
delegated children running.

**Lineage is the `spawned_by` graph.** Each agent records the parent DID that
authorized its spawn (`spawned_by`) plus its `depth` (hops from the root) — the
same lineage the ledger and the [`thoth.fleet.swarm.v1`](#commands) frame already
carry (see [Recursive delegation](#recursive-delegation)). The set of an agent's
descendants is the **recursive closure** over that graph: the agent, its
children, its children's children, all the way down.

**Reap or breach cascades down the closure.** A `reap --cascade` reaps the target
**and** every node in its spawned subtree; an **enforced oath-breach
auto-cascades** the same way — a breached agent's spawned descendants are reaped
too. The blast radius is exactly the subtree and no wider, and it is bounded and
provable:

- **Each cascaded reap is a signed `Reap` audit entry.** Every node the cascade
  reaps appends a hash-chained, root-signed `reap` entry to the
  [audit chain](#tamper-evident-audit-chain), so the containment is recorded
  per-node and the chain **still verifies** afterward (`audit --verify` stays
  `ok`). The cascade is auditable end to end: the set of reaped ids is exactly
  the spawned closure of the target, witnessed in the chain.
- **It ties to the trust model.** Trust attaches to the
  [`spawned_by`](#trust) identity, so the same lineage that propagates a child's
  breach *up* to its parent is the lineage the cascade reaps *down* — a parent
  cannot be held accountable for children it is allowed to abandon. Containment
  closes that gap: revoking a parent revokes the authority of everything below
  it, matching the chain rule that a revocation at any link kills everything
  beneath it.

Plain `reap` (no `--cascade`) keeps the single-agent behavior — only the named
agent is revoked. `--cascade` opts into the subtree closure.

```
monster-swarm reap --cascade --ucan <token> --id <id> [--json]
    Reap the agent AND its entire spawned subtree (recursive closure over the
    spawned_by lineage graph). Each reaped node emits a signed Reap audit entry,
    so the chain still verifies afterward. The two-queen floor still protects the
    last persistent breeder. With --json, emits { "reaped": ["<id>", ...],
    "count": <N> } — the ids reaped (the spawned closure) and the total.
```

An enforced `oath-breach` auto-cascades a breached agent's spawned descendants on
the same path, each emitting its own `Reap` entry — so a rogue node cannot leave
live delegated children behind it. Surfaced over MCP as the `subagent_reap`
tool's `cascade` arg in [`monster-resident`](../monster-resident). Full operator
detail in the
[`MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK`](../MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK.md#cascade-revocation).

## User-spawnable

The human is now a first-class spawner, peer to Thoth: the same `spawn` runs
under a user's own UCAN. Bootstrap once with
[`scripts/grant-user.sh`](./scripts/grant-user.sh) (keygen +
`monster-resident grant --with agent:fleet --can agent/spawn` → writes
`~/.edgework/agent.ucan`), then spawn via
[`moonshine agent spawn`](../moonshine/README.md#agent-spawn-user-spawnable-agents),
which shells to `monster-swarm spawn` under that capability. An agent is one
definition regardless of who minted it — leased process + own DID + qspec (works)
+ oath (conduct) — spawnable by Thoth **or** the user.

## Commands

```
monster-swarm whoami
    Print the shared resident root DID (must root every capability).

monster-swarm spawn  --ucan <token> --node <bin> [--id <id>] [--caste scout|breeder]
                     [--caps net,fs] [--lease <secs>] [--max <secs>]
                     [--grant-ttl <secs>] [-- <node args...>]
    Verify, admit, delegate, and launch a leased subagent. <node> resolves
    against $DI_BIN_DIR or the distributed-inference build outputs (e.g.
    `aeon-monitor`), or is used as a path.

monster-swarm delegate-spawn --ucan <parent-token> --issuer-secret <hex>
                             --to <child-did> --caps <subset> [--ttl <secs>]
    Mint an ATTENUATED agent/spawn capability for a child (constraint
    {caps: subset}), so a parent can let a child spawn within — never beyond —
    its own caps. verify_chain rejects a non-subset; ops::spawn enforces the
    requested sandbox caps stay within the authorizing chain (root agent/* is
    unrestricted, a delegated holder is bounded). Delegation attenuates: no
    escalation down the tree. (See Recursive delegation above.)

monster-swarm list   [--json]
    Show the live fleet (local read).

monster-swarm update --ucan <token> --id <id> [--caps net,fs] [--lease <secs>] [--grant-ttl <secs>]
    Re-mint the command grant (renew / re-attenuate caps). NOTE: a running
    node's sandbox caps are fixed at spawn; cap changes apply to the next
    launch — reap + respawn to tighten a live agent.

monster-swarm reap   --ucan <token> --id <id> [--cascade] [--json]
    Two-queen-floor-checked revoke; the node dies at the next lease tick. With
    --cascade, also reaps the agent's entire spawned subtree (recursive closure
    over the spawned_by lineage graph), each a signed Reap audit entry; --json
    emits { "reaped": [...], "count": N }. (See Cascade revocation above.)

monster-swarm oath-breach --ucan <token> --id <id> --reason <reason>
    Enforce an oath breach: revoke the agent (lease killed at the next tick) and
    append a record to $MONSTER_GUARD_HOME/breaches.json (append-only audit).

monster-swarm breaches [--json]
    List the recorded oath breaches (read the audit log).

monster-swarm trust  [--json]
    List spawner DIDs with their trust score and recent breach count. Trust
    attaches to the spawner (stable identity), not the agent (fresh DID per
    spawn): trust_of(spawner) = clamp(1 - 0.34 * recent_breach_count, 0..1).
    ops::spawn denies a spawn when the requesting spawner's trust is below a
    floor (default 0.34, env MONSTER_SWARM_MIN_TRUST) — repeat offenders are
    locked out; clean spawners (1.0) are unaffected.

monster-swarm vibe   [--dwell-ms <ms>] [--json]
    Read the swarm partyline and emit the Thoth `thoth.fleet.swarm.v1` frame
    (the fleet body/consciousness) summarizing the collective vibe.

monster-swarm broadcast --consciousness <0-5> [--energy <n>] [--recalibration]
                        [--id <source_id>] [--hz <rate>] [--secs <duration>]
                        [--count <n>] [--json]
    Emit SwarmSignal(s) onto the UDP partyline the live `auto` controller reads
    (the driver side of the autonomic loop). Broadcast HOT (consciousness >=
    --bud-at, default 4) or --recalibration to provoke a BUD; broadcast CALM
    (0) to provoke a vent/REAP. --count emits N signals; --hz/--secs sustain a
    stream. With --json, prints exactly
    { "emitted": <N>, "consciousness": <C>, "source_id": "<src>" } on stdout
    (status to stderr). See Autonomic re-fork below.

monster-swarm auto   --ucan <token> [--node <bin>] [--bud-at <n>] [--reap-below <n>]
                     [--min-live <n>] [--caps net,fs] [--lease <secs>]
                     [--dwell-ms <ms>] [--interval <secs>]
                     [--enforce-oaths] [--breach-at <n>]
    One autonomic homeostasis tick, or run it as a live controller loop with
    --interval <secs>: each tick reads the collective consciousness off the
    partyline and buds a scout when hot / reaps an idle scout when calm. With
    --enforce-oaths, also reap+record agents whose conduct signal (consciousness
    >= --breach-at, default 5) has crossed into breach. Every action it takes is
    a signed audit entry tagged actor "auto". See Autonomic re-fork below.

monster-swarm reconcile
    Prune ledger rows whose supervisor process has died (liveness sync).
```

## Autonomic re-fork (live)

`auto --interval <secs>` runs as a **live controller loop**: a daemon that reads
the collective swarm consciousness off the [partyline](#state--wire) every tick
and **homeostatically re-forks the fleet within the FRF budget**. It is the
closed loop that lets the swarm self-regulate on a real box with no operator in
the path — the AntColony re-fork running for real, not just a single tick.

Each tick:

- **hot** — collective consciousness `>= --bud-at` (default 4), or a node fired a
  recalibration `=> **bud a scout**. The extra reproductive void lifts the race
  mode (`extra_void_increases_race`): under load the colony grows.
- **calm** — collective consciousness `<= --reap-below` (default 0) and the fleet
  is over-provisioned `=> **reap the oldest idle scout** (the vent), never below
  `--min-live`, never a breeder.
- otherwise **hold** (the hysteresis dead band between the two thresholds).

The loop is **doubly bounded** by the [AntColony FRF budget](#the-two-guardrails):
it can never bud past the **DECAGON ceiling** (default 10) or breach the
**two-queen reap floor**, and the **God-Formula stability weight**
(`budget - min(vent, budget) + 1`) it reports is provably always `>= 1`. It also
acts only within the **UCAN** it is handed (`agent/*` on `agent:fleet`) — never
beyond it. The decision (`auto::decide`) is pure and exhaustively tested.

**Every autonomic action is audited.** Each bud / reap / breach the controller
takes appends a signed entry to the
[tamper-evident audit chain](#tamper-evident-audit-chain) **tagged actor `auto`**,
so the autonomic actions are distinguishable in the record from operator- or
Thoth-driven ones — the loop is accountable exactly like a hand-driven mutation.

**Drive and observe it with `broadcast`.** The controller is the *reader* of the
partyline; [`broadcast`](#commands) is the *writer*. Emit a hot signal to provoke
a bud, a calm signal to provoke a vent/reap, and watch the result land in the
audit chain:

```
# Terminal A — run the controller as a daemon (a 30 s loop):
monster-swarm auto --ucan <token> --node aeon-monitor --interval 30

# Terminal B — drive the partyline to exercise it:
monster-swarm broadcast --consciousness 5 --json     # hot  -> next tick BUDs
monster-swarm broadcast --consciousness 0 --json     # calm -> next tick REAPs an idle scout
monster-swarm broadcast --recalibration --json       # storm -> BUD regardless of level

# Read back the autonomic actions (entries tagged actor "auto"):
monster-swarm audit --json
```

Over MCP, drive it with the `swarm_broadcast` tool and read it with `audit_log`
in [`monster-resident`](../monster-resident); `swarm_autotick` runs a single tick
on a caller-chosen cadence (the same decision, one step at a time).

**Still next:** wire the [zedge](../zedge) passthrough so the controller can also
re-fork across the edge mesh, and ship the CI-cheap dense 0.5B knot so the Paris
ratchet runs in CI (steps in the runbook).

## Autonomic homeostasis (the AntColony re-fork)

`auto` closes the loop so the swarm self-regulates. A node's consciousness is its
inner-vent failure streak (`distributed_inference::conscious_swarm_node`); at the
fleet scale:

- collective consciousness **hot** (≥ `--bud-at`, default 4) or a node fired a
  recalibration ⇒ **bud a scout** (extra reproductive void lifts the race mode,
  `extra_void_increases_race`);
- collective consciousness **calm** (≤ `--reap-below`, default 0) and the fleet
  is over-provisioned ⇒ **reap the oldest idle scout** (never below `--min-live`,
  never a breeder).

The loop is doubly bounded: it acts only within the **UCAN** it is handed, and
budding is gated by the **FRF population ceiling** — it can never exceed the
manifold or breach the two-queen floor. The decision (`auto::decide`) is pure and
exhaustively tested; drive ticks on any cadence (`--interval`, cron, or the
`swarm_autotick` MCP tool). For running it as a **live controller daemon** — and
driving/observing it with `broadcast` — see
[Autonomic re-fork (live)](#autonomic-re-fork-live) above.

## Subagent identity

Each subagent gets its own DID (not just the monster-guard grant). On `spawn` a
fresh keypair is minted; the **DID** is recorded in the ledger (audit + shown in
the fleet frame), and the **secret** is written to a `0600` keyfile under
`agents/<id>.key` — never to the ledger, never to the environment. The node is
handed `MONSTER_AGENT_ID`, `MONSTER_AGENT_DID`, and `MONSTER_AGENT_KEYFILE` (the
path) so the node signs its swarm broadcasts / orchestrate callbacks with its own
P-256 key, and the receiver verifies each signature against the ledger DID (the
ECDSA verify + sign loop is closed; the subagent is its own identity, not merely
the monster-guard grant). Keyfiles are removed on `reap` and `reconcile`.

## Tamper-evident audit chain

Trust and breaches give the fleet a *memory*; the **audit chain** gives that
memory a **tamper-evident spine**. Every lifecycle event — `spawn`, `update`,
`reap`, `delegate`, `breach` — is appended to a **hash-chained, root-signed**
log, `audit.jsonl`, so the record of what the fleet did cannot be quietly edited
after the fact.

Each entry links to the one before it and is independently signed:

- **Hash-chained.** Every entry carries `prev_hash` (the `hash` of the prior
  entry) and its own `hash` (over the entry's content plus that `prev_hash`).
  The entries form a linked chain rooted at a genesis link — change one byte of
  any entry and every `hash` from that point forward no longer matches.
- **Root-signed.** Each entry is **ECDSA-signed by the fleet root key** (the
  shared resident root, the same authority that roots every capability). A
  forged entry cannot carry a valid `sig`, so it is rejected even if its hashes
  are internally consistent.

Together these make five classes of corruption detectable: **modification**
(a changed field breaks the entry hash), **insertion** and **deletion** (a
`prev_hash`/`hash` link no longer matches its neighbour), **reordering** (the
chain links no longer line up in sequence), and **forgery** (a fabricated entry
has no valid root signature). `audit --verify` walks the chain and catches all
five.

### The audit entry

Each line of `audit.jsonl` is one JSON object:

```json
{
  "seq": 7,
  "ts": "<timestamp>",
  "kind": "spawn",
  "subject": "<agent id>",
  "actor": "<spawner / reporter DID>",
  "detail": { },
  "prev_hash": "<hash of entry seq 6>",
  "hash": "<this entry's hash>",
  "sig": "<root ECDSA signature over this entry>"
}
```

`kind` is the lifecycle event (`spawn` / `update` / `reap` / `delegate` /
`breach`); `subject` is the agent the event is about; `actor` is who caused it;
`detail` carries event-specific fields. `prev_hash` / `hash` are the chain
links and `sig` is the root signature.

### Commands

```
monster-swarm audit
    Print the audit chain (human-readable).

monster-swarm audit --json
    Emit the chain as JSON: { "entries": [ { seq, ts, kind, subject, actor,
    detail, prev_hash, hash, sig }, ... ] } — the raw, append-only record.

monster-swarm audit --verify
    Walk the chain and report whether it is intact (human-readable).

monster-swarm audit --verify --json
    Walk the chain and emit the verdict as JSON:
        { "ok": <bool>, "entries": <N>, "first_bad": <null|seq>,
          "reason": "<why>", "head": "<hash of the last entry>" }
    Exit code 0 when the chain verifies; exit code 3 when tampering is detected
    (the first broken link is reported in first_bad / reason).
```

The exit codes make `audit --verify` usable as a tripwire: a `0` is a clean
chain, a `3` is "the record has been tampered with" — fail closed on it.

### Surfaced to Thoth

The verified state of the chain rides **front of mind** in the
[`thoth.fleet.swarm.v1`](#commands) fleet frame as
`audit: { head, verified, entries }` — the chain `head` hash, whether it
currently `verified`, and the entry count — so Thoth always sees the
tamper-evident root of the fleet's history alongside the live vibe, the sworn
oaths, and the trust scores. The frame is the place the whole fleet body is
visible at a glance; the audit object is the integrity witness inside it.

The chain is **append-only** — like `breaches.json`, it is never rewritten, only
grown — and is surfaced over MCP through the `audit_verify` and `audit_log`
tools in [`monster-resident`](../monster-resident). Full operator detail (where
`audit.jsonl` lives, how to verify, what a failure means) is in the
[`MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK`](../MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK.md#tamper-evident-audit-chain).

## State & wire

State is shared with monster-guard / monster-resident under
`$MONSTER_GUARD_HOME` (default `~/.moonshine.d`): `root.key`, `revocations.json`,
`fleet.json` (this crate's ledger, `0600`, written atomically), `agents/<id>.key`
(`0600` per-subagent secrets), `audit.jsonl` (the append-only, hash-chained,
root-signed audit chain — see [Tamper-evident audit chain](#tamper-evident-audit-chain)),
and `logs/<name>.log` (node stdout/stderr). The
`monster-guard` binary is located via `$MONSTER_GUARD_BIN`, the sibling build
output, or PATH. Nodes are resolved against the **allowlist**
(`$MONSTER_SWARM_NODES` colon-separated, and/or `$DI_BIN_DIR`) — a holder of
`agent/spawn` can only launch binaries inside it, never an arbitrary path.

`SwarmSignal` is bincode wire-compatible with
`distributed_inference::pleromatic_frame::SwarmSignal` and the UDP partyline in
`swarm_net` (`255.255.255.255:4242`). The exact byte layout is pinned by a
round-trip test — if it drifts, the swarm goes deaf.

## Security

See [`SECURITY.md`](./SECURITY.md) for the threat model, the controls (UCAN
admission, FRF budget, node allowlist, lease/revoke, secret handling), and the
accepted residual risks.

## Build

```
cargo test          # 27 unit tests (budget, ledger, swarm wire, auto, guard allowlist, ops, sign) + signing_loop
cargo build --release
```

The **Qwen quality gate** (`tests/paris_subagent.rs`) is a separate, heavy,
self-skipping integration test: it spawns a real `fat-station` subagent and
asserts the mesh predicts ` Paris` (token 12095) for "The capital of France is".
It skips (passes) on plain `cargo test` unless a dense Qwen knot and release
builds of `fat-station` + `monster-guard` are present. To run the real ratchet
(~2 min), see the [`MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK`](../MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK.md):

```
cargo test --release --test paris_subagent -- --nocapture   # look for "[paris] OK"
```

## Benchmarks

A dated, append-only benchmark ledger lives in the
[`MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK`](../MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK.md#benchmarks-cold-vs-warm)
(inference, quality ratchet, data movement, parallel-wave wall). On this mesh the
**page-cache state dominates wall time**, so every number is labeled **cold**
(uncached 4.7 GB knot read) vs **warm** (knot page-cached). Snapshot
(2026-05-22, Apple Silicon, CPU-only, `qwen-coder-7b.knot` 4.7 GB):

| Op | Cold | Warm |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Paris gate (5 sequential `/decode-next`) | 123.89 s | — |
| `paris-probe-rknot` full forward (5-token, 28 layers) | ~25 s | ~5–6 s |
| fat-station boot to `/health` | longer (uncached read) | ~2 s |
| `cargo test --lib` (27 tests) | — | 2.88 s |

Quality ratchet (real weights, top-1 next token): `The capital of France is` ->
` Paris` (12095), `The capital of Japan is` -> ` Tokyo` (26194), `The opposite
of hot is` -> ` cold` (9255), `import numpy as` -> ` np` (2595). Add new dated
blocks to the runbook to track the trend over time.

### Admission CLI (cold vs warm)

The admission surface itself is cheap. `scripts/bench-admission.sh` times the
short CRUD/admission ops with the **median of N runs** (default 5; set
`BENCH_RUNS`), reporting **cold** (a fresh `MONSTER_GUARD_HOME` per run — no
`root.key`, no ledger, no warm identity) vs **warm** (repeated against an
initialized home). It does NOT time the heavy fat-station inference (that is the
Paris gate above). Snapshot (2026-05-23, Apple Silicon, **local CPU**, darwin
arm64, median of 15):

| Op | Cold (ms) | Warm (ms) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `whoami` | 17 | 18 |
| `spawn` (oath-admitted roundtrip) | 23 | 25 |
| `delegate-spawn` (attenuated UCAN) | 18 | 24 |
| `audit --verify` (chain recompute) | 12 | 11 |
| `reap --cascade` (3-node subtree) | 29 | 34 |
| `auto` (1 tick, quiet partyline) | 32 | 35 |

(The `auto` row was measured the same day under wave load / disk near full, so it
reads a touch higher than the idle-box rows above; on an idle box it lands in the
same ~20 ms band as `whoami`/`spawn`.)

These ops never read the 4.7 GB knot, so unlike the inference numbers they are
**not page-cache-bound**: cold and warm sit within run-to-run jitter (tens of
ms), dominated by process spawn / exec and per-call ECDSA, not by disk state. The
big cold/warm gap on this mesh shows up in the knot-reading inference path, not
here. `audit --verify` recomputes the whole hash chain and verifies every root
signature, yet stays in the same jitter band: the chains are short and sha256 +
P-256 verify are cheap, so integrity checking is effectively free to run as a
tripwire. `reap --cascade` (lineage containment: revoke a target AND its entire
spawned subtree) sits slightly higher because it walks the `spawned_by` closure
and emits one signed `Reap` per member; for a 3-node subtree it is still in the
same low-ms band, so cascade containment is cheap enough to fire on every
oath-breach. `auto` (one autonomic tick reading a **quiet** partyline, so it
HOLDs — no bud, no reap, `--dwell-ms 0` to exclude the partyline listen window)
is the loop's pure read+decide overhead: it sits in the same band as the other
admission ops, so the swarm can self-regulate on a tight interval at negligible
cost. The cascade and auto rows only appear when their CLIs are built; otherwise
the harness skips them cleanly.

Run it yourself:

```
bash scripts/bench-admission.sh            # median of 5
BENCH_RUNS=15 bash scripts/bench-admission.sh
```
